{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks for a poison that produces bluish discoloration of the stomach during postmortem examination. Among the given options, oxalic acid is known to cause a characteristic greenish-black discoloration of the stomach and intestines due to the formation of insoluble calcium oxalate. However, the question specifically mentions a bluish discoloration, which is more indicative of arsenic poisoning. Arsenic causes a blue-black discoloration of the stomach and intestines during postmortem examination. None of the other options (Sodium amytal, Soneryl) are known to cause this specific discoloration.", "answer_choice": "D"}
